executive_summary.main_recommendation: Pain points first: monthly AI spend rose from 55203.62 USD in 2024-10 to 77593.63 USD in 2025-03, up 40.6% over the period, with increases in 4 of 5 month-to-month periods and a peak of 82155.81 USD in 2025-02. This means AI spend is not broadly wasteful, but it is unevenly productive: a small group is consuming very large token volumes without matching delivery, while a separate group is turning low token use into strong delivery and should be copied. The clearest cost-control cases are Omar Leclerc, Baptiste Bernard, Ines Camus, and Adrien Ivanova: tokens per story point means how many tokens were spent to deliver one story point, and lower is better; Omar is at 18386094, Baptiste at 18269640, Ines at 10328058, and Adrien at 10069291 versus a team median of 4285456, so cap usage, review prompt logs, reduce oversized context, and retrain before approving more budget. The best workflows to replicate are Leila Park, Jules Nguyen, Maya Kowalski, Amina Blanc, and Victor Durand: story points per million tokens means how much delivery came from one million tokens, and higher is better; Jules is at 8.26, Leila at 4.83, Maya at 2.92, Amina at 1.61, and Victor at 1.63 versus a team median of 0.24, so recognize them and have them coach peers on prompt discipline, smaller context, and repeatable task patterns. The under-adoption group is Zoe Laurent, Nadia Fernandez, and Antoine Petit: they are all below the median on both tokens_used and story_points, and Zoe and Antoine are also well below the median on tickets_resolved with slower time_to_completion_days, so this is a coaching and enablement issue more than a budget-cut issue. For next month, hold total spend near the lower forecast of 73284.79 USD by cutting overspender usage first, while tracking whether coached low-adoption users raise story_points and tickets_resolved and whether efficient users can spread their workflow across the team.

employee_metrics.name: Leila Park
employee_metrics.recommendation: Leila Park is a model to copy: she used 28132738 tokens and delivered 136 story points, or 206858 tokens per story point, versus a team median of 4285456. She also resolved 22 tickets versus a team median of 9 and finished work in 1.5 days versus 2.29 days. Praise this behavior and ask her to mentor peers on prompt discipline, smaller context, and how she turns AI use into steady delivery. Next-month KPI to watch: story_points_per_million_tokens.

employee_metrics.name: Amina Blanc
employee_metrics.recommendation: Amina Blanc used 40417346 tokens for 65 story points, or 1.61 story points per million tokens versus a team median of 0.24, showing strong efficiency with lower-than-median token use. She also resolved 14 tickets and had 23 merge requests, both above the team median. Replicate her workflow and ask her to coach peers on prompt structure, context selection, and task patterns that keep token use controlled. Next-month KPI to watch: story_points_per_million_tokens.

employee_metrics.name: Camille Garnier
employee_metrics.recommendation: Camille Garnier is efficient: top 42.5% story point delivery (rank #17/40, P60) with top 75% token usage (rank #30/40, P28), producing 1.17 story points per million tokens versus a team median of 0.24. Because token usage is -70.5% vs median while efficiency is +395.2% vs median, praise this usage pattern. Delivery context: 8 tickets resolved versus a team median of 9, 2.38 days average completion time versus 2.29 days median, 2 bugs closed, 14 merge requests, and 5208 lines of code. Have this employee share prompts, context-selection habits, and task patterns with the rest of the team.

He matched the team median on tickets resolved with far lower token use than almost everyone else. Replicate his workflow and have him coach peers on concise prompts and avoiding oversized context. Next-month KPI to watch: story_points.
